description: Explicit mention of dish prices in Saudi Riyals within food content captions, typically formatted as 'السعر: X ريال' to provide direct cost transparency for menu items.
structural_recipe: Include the dish name followed by explicit price notation in Arabic (السعر: [number] ريال). Place price information after describing the dish characteristics and location. Format consistently with currency symbol or text (ريال). Keep price clearly separated and easy to scan visually within the caption.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers value price transparency and comparison shopping, particularly in F&B. Stating prices upfront builds trust, reduces friction in the consideration phase, and enables followers to quickly assess value proposition. Price disclosure demonstrates confidence in product positioning and caters to the practical decision-making culture.
transplantation_caveats: Price sensitivity varies significantly by neighborhood and consumer segment - state prices only if positioned competitively
Avoid displaying prices for premium/luxury positioning where explicit cost mention may diminish perceived exclusivity
Currency display (ريال vs SAR symbol) affects perception - test format preference with target audience
Price volatility in F&B may quickly render captions inaccurate - establish update cadence protocols
